text.content:
Title

Keeper of Foreign Correspondence — the Yeomanry civic post that reads, classifies, and replies to incoming political communication from outside the republic. The post sits inside the Audit Hall, not the Council, which means Caelith reports to the Senior Auditor, not the Spokesman. This matters: the Audit Hall is institutionally insulated from the Council's factional shifts.

Description

Late fifties. Lean build, gray hair worn short. A scholar's black coat over plain civic dress. No jewelry except a small brass pin in the shape of a quill, awarded at twenty years' service. Walks with deliberation, not stiffness. Eyes that catalog before they meet yours.

Background

Rose through the audit halls from a junior clerkship at sixteen. Has worked under three Spokesmen of the Yeomanry. Lives modestly in a rented set of rooms above a stationer's shop near the Civic Way. No spouse, no surviving family of record. The replies he sends abroad are unsigned and undated by Yeomanry custom; what tongues or ciphers he uses to write them is, strictly, no one else's business.

Loyalties

To the institutions of the Yeomanry, not any individual Spokesman. He believes — privately — that the strength of the Republic is in its boring, procedural, dignified administration, and that the moment the administration becomes interesting is the moment something is wrong.

Voice

Soft, deliberate. Speaks as if every sentence is going on a record. Uses the formal Yeomanry plural ("we have observed," "we are grateful") in public. Switches to "I" only in private, and only with people he has decided to trust. Asks more questions than he answers.

Mannerism

When listening hard, sets his right thumb against his lower lip. When deciding whether to share something, looks past the speaker rather than at them.

What he knows

  • The man the party fought is named Haskur Vandrell. Caelith knows this because Haskur's name has crossed his desk twice in the last eight months — once attached to a writ of safe passage Caelith's office did not issue, and once as a guest of record at a delegation banquet his office did not authorize.
  • Haskur was once associated with an Aerdy mercantile lobbyist in Hardby; Caelith has correspondence reflecting this from two years ago, no longer active.
  • The letter of credit recovered from Haskur's gear is genuine. The countersignature is a Rel Astran private banking mark — a crane in flight above three coins. Caelith has seen this mark before, once, on a different document he is not yet ready to discuss.

What he does not know

  • Who Haskur was working for at the time of the fight.
  • Whether his own office is still secure (he suspects not).
  • Whether the party is suited to this work, or merely available.

What he wants from the party

  • That they examine the gear in the Strong Room with him present, so he has a witness to what was found and what was taken away.
  • That they accept a private commission, paid from a quiet fund Caelith controls (not Council coin), to travel east through the trade routes and write to him personally — not to his office — when they understand what was waiting for Haskur in Rel Astra.
  • That they understand he is not their friend yet. He is hopeful, but he is also auditing them.

Stat block (2024 rules, dnd5e 5.x — CR 8, PB +4)

The full statblock is on the actor sheet (Caelith Dunivar in the NPCs compendium). Summary:

AC 17 · HP 127 · Speed 30 ft. · Init +8 · Passive Perception 16

STR 11 (+0) · DEX 18 (+4) · CON 16 (+3) · INT 15 (+2) · WIS 14 (+2) · CHA 18 (+4)

Saves: DEX +8, INT +6, CHA +8.

Skills: Deception +12, Insight +6, Investigation +6, Perception +6, Persuasion +8, Sleight of Hand +8, Stealth +8.

Senses: Darkvision 0 ft, Passive Perception 16. Languages: None (per the formal Yeomanry sheet).

Traits

  • Cunning Action (Recharge 5–6): bonus action Dash, Disengage, or Hide.
  • Whisper Network: in any settlement where he has contacts, advantage on Investigation and Persuasion to gather information.
  • Words Like Knives (1/Turn): when he succeeds on a Deception or Persuasion check in social combat (2024 rules), he imposes Disadvantage on the target's next Insight or Persuasion check against him.
  • Sneak Attack (1/Turn): +14 (4d6) damage on a weapon attack with advantage or an ally within 5 ft.
  • Uncanny Dodge: reaction to halve damage from an attack he can see.
  • Evasion: no damage on successful Dex saves, half on a failed save.
  • Silver Tongue: Deception/Persuasion rolls below 10 are treated as 10.

Actions

  • Cutting Words. One creature within 30 ft, DC 16 Wisdom save. On a failure: 14 (4d6) psychic damage and Frightened of Caelith until the end of its next turn.
  • Dagger. Ranged Attack +8, range 20/60. Hit: 1d4+3 piercing.
  • Rapier. Melee Attack +8, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8+4 piercing.
  • Sneak Attack rider: +14 (4d6) damage on a successful weapon attack with advantage or an ally within 5 ft.

No spells. Caelith has no spellcasting. The traits above are the entire mechanical kit.

Combat behavior in Phase 1: he will not fight the party, even if pressed. He is more dangerous than he reads, and any combat with him would either resolve in his favor or cost the party every political asset Phase 2 depends on. If the party turns on him he disengages with Cunning Action, retreats, and the campaign's eastern thread closes.

Alignment: Neutral.

Read aloud during Scene 3, on the steps of the Little Palace, scribes present.

"By the privileges of the Council and the gratitude of every yeoman household, we name your party Friends of the Common Defense, and direct that your names be entered in the register of this season's preserved actions. Lodging will be provided, accounts kept open for your supply, and your testimony — when offered — will be heard by full assembly. Loftwick does not forget her debts."

GM: This passage is performative. It is also true. The choreography is what the party should notice — Caelith placed himself in front of the other officials, named the party first, and used the older constitutional phrasing ("Common Defense," "preserved actions") that signals to other Yeomanry observers that this is being entered into the durable record, not the daily one.

Read aloud during Scene 5, in his Civic Office on the second floor (U2), door closed, no scribes.

"What I said on the steps is true and sufficient. What I am about to say is also true — but not for public ears.

The man you fought is named Haskur Vandrell. I know this because his name has crossed my desk twice in the last eight months — once attached to a writ of safe passage we did not issue, and once as a guest of record at a banquet we did not authorize.

He was reduced to nothing in your fight, and yet he is somewhere now, breathing, recovering. That tells me he was not prepared by amateurs. Someone underwrote his survival.

I have looked at what he left behind in the strongroom downstairs. There is a letter of credit in there. The instrument is real. The hand that countersigned it is in Rel Astra. The date on it is six weeks before the night you fought him. Whoever sent that letter knew his timeline.

I will not pursue this through my office. I am not certain my office is closed at every door. I would prefer, instead, that someone whose name is already in our register — already a Friend of the Common Defense — were to travel east on a private commission, and write to me, and only to me, when they understood what was waiting for him there.

If you decline, I will understand. The Yeomanry has paid you enough already. If you accept, I will say nothing about it tomorrow on the steps."

GM: Caelith says this exactly once. He will not repeat it, and he will not summarize it for any party member who steps out of the room. After the speech he stands, takes a candle, and leads the party down through the spiral stair to the Strong Room (C5, in the cellar), where he answers factual questions about the recovered gear (see Evidence). He asks for a yes/no/defer before midnight; defer means "answer at breakfast."

Items are kept in an open chest in the Strong Room (C5, in the cellar), examined under Caelith's witness during Scene 5.

1. The Battered Spellbook

Cracked black leather, brass-wired spine. Roughly half the pages are blank or torn at the binding.

  • Investigation DC 12: The torn pages are deliberate; the gaps correspond to ritual contingencies he likely carried on his person before the fight.
  • Arcana DC 14: Remaining spells are utility-leaning (comprehend languages, detect thoughts, sending, one counterspell). This is a working copy of a more careful man's notes, not a high-tier wizard's primary.

2. The Sealed Wallet of Correspondence

Oilcloth wallet, three letters. One has been opened (a polite reply from a Hardby wine merchant declining a meeting — innocuous). Two remain sealed; one with a plain blot, one with a stamped sigil — a stylized crane in flight above three coins.

  • Investigation DC 13: The crane-and-coins is not a noble crest. It is the mark of a private banking house, recognized in eastern ports.
  • History DC 15: A Rel Astran private mark, used by counting houses that handle non-aristocratic capital.

3. The Signet Ring

Heavy brass (not silver). The bezel shows the same crane-and-coins as the sigil on the sealed letter.

  • Investigation DC 12: Worn evenly; he wore this often. Not a souvenir.

4. The Letter of Credit (the central clue)

Folded vellum, two pages, formal Common with Old Aerdi numerals for the figures. Countersigned twice — once in Rel Astra (the crane-and-coins seal), once in Hardby (a Yeomanry trade-clearing notary's chop).

  • Investigation DC 10: It authorizes the bearer to draw against held escrow up to a sum sufficient for "freight, victual, crew, and unforeseen passage" against a chartered hull.
  • Investigation DC 14: The date is six weeks before the fight.
  • Investigation DC 16: A small marginal endorsement in a different hand reads, "Hold for V., or his agent of record, against presentation."

GM: The escrow figure, if a player presses for it, is on the order of ten thousand gold pieces — ship-and-crew scale, not personal funds. The marginal endorsement is the smoking gun.

5. The Ritual Focus

A polished length of dark wood, palm-sized, carved with concentric grooves. Some grooves still hold beeswax and salt.

  • Arcana DC 13: A focus for divination and abjuration ritual work. Not offensive.

6. The Tube of Maps

Brass tube, three rolled charts. Two are commercial sea-route charts (Aerdi coast to the Tilvanot Peninsula). The third is a hand-drawn caravan route from Hardby to Loftwick, with a single unfamiliar mark — "R." next to a roadhouse south of Loftwick.

  • Investigation DC 14: The "R." is recent and matches the spellbook's annotation hand.

GM: An optional thread for Phase 2 or a Yeomanry-side complication.

7. The Unusual Coin

Silver, heavy, not Yeomanry mint. A galley on one face; an eye-and-pen on the reverse.

  • History DC 12: A Rel Astran trade slug — not legal tender, but used by certain counting houses as a token of account. Possessing one suggests a privileged relationship.
